```python
class Solution:
    def getMinSwaps(self, num: str, k: int) -> int:
        from itertools import permutations
        from collections import deque
        perms = sorted([int(''.join(p)) for p in permutations(num)])
        target = sorted([p for p in perms if p > int(num)])[k-1]
        target = str(target)
        num = list(num)
        res = 0
        for i in range(len(num)):
            if num[i] != target[i]:
                for j in range(i+1, len(num)):
                    if num[j] == target[i]:
                        res += j - i
                        num[i:j+1] = [num[j]] + num[i:j]
                        break
        return res
```
efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(n^2)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: MEDIUM
explanation: The solution generates all permutations of the input number, sorts them, and finds the kth smallest wonderful integer. It then uses a two-pointer approach to find the minimum number of adjacent digit swaps needed to reach the target number.
